commands.review_summary:
The PR changes CodexBar localization selection so System language mode uses macOS preferred languages and locale-variant `.lproj` fallback candidates, with regression tests for Chinese variant selection.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main has Chinese localization resources but System mode and lproj lookup do not explicitly handle locale variants such as `zh-Hans-CN`. I did not run tests because this review is required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer waiver, not a narrow code repair ClawSweeper can supply.

target.title: Fix System language fallback for locale variants
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes a first-launch localization issue where CodexBar could show English even when macOS is Chinese and app language is set to **System**.

## Root cause
System locale variants like `zh-Hans-CN` could miss existing resources such as `zh-Hans.lproj`, causing fallback to English.

## Changes
- Use `Bundle.preferredLocalizations(from:forPreferences:)` with `Locale.preferredLanguages` in System mode.
- Expand lproj candidates with a fallback chain:
  - `zh-Hans-CN -> zh-Hans -> zh`
  - normalize `_` to `-`
- Add regression coverage in `LocalizationBundleTests`.

## Validation
- `swift test --filter LocalizationBundleTests`
- 7/7 passed
target.author: yuxin-qiao
